content.claim: Comparative cases (Finland 1970s, France age-11-to-13 delay, Poland 1999) show delayed tracking improved equity and attainment, but only when bundled with unified national curricula, extended compulsory comprehensive schooling, and coordinated curricular reform—not as isolated interventions
content.context: Fresh live research through the Portuguese system reform lens
limitations: Bundle design prevents isolation of tracking delay from curriculum unification effects
Different historical and institutional contexts reduce direct comparability to Hungary
No Hungary-specific package has been modeled or piloted alongside a hypothetical gimnázium delay
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
